sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: Position Size = Account Capital / (Maximum Loss Per Unit / f). Optimal f derived from historical HPR analysis. Example: 50,000 / (5,000 / f) where f is determined from trade history. Max loss per position capped at 5,000 (10% of 50k capital).

source_rule_text: Position size is determined by dividing total account capital by the ratio of maximum loss per unit to optimal f, expressed as the formula: Capital / (MaxLoss / f) = Position Size
